title: Identifying a congruence test
problem: Triangles $ABC$ and $DEF$ have $AB = DE = 8$ cm, $BC = EF = 6$ cm, and $AC = DF = 10$ cm. Which congruence test proves $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle DEF$?

steps.working: $AB = DE = 8$ cm, $BC = EF = 6$ cm, $AC = DF = 10$ cm

steps.instruction: Count the pairs: three pairs of equal sides, no angles given.
steps.working: Three sides of one triangle equal three sides of the other.
Three pairs of equal sides ‚Üí SSS (Side-Side-Side)

title: Finding a missing angle using congruence
problem: Triangle $PQR \cong$ triangle $XYZ$. $\angle P = 50¬∞$ and $\angle Q = 80¬∞$. Find $\angle Z$.

steps.instruction: Find the third angle of $\triangle PQR$ using the angle sum.
Use the correspondence in the congruence statement: $P \leftrightarrow X$, $Q \leftrightarrow Y$, $R \leftrightarrow Z$.

title: Proving congruence in a rectangle and finding an angle
problem: In rectangle $ABCD$, diagonal $AC$ is drawn. Prove that $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle CDA$, then find $\angle DCA$ given that $\angle BAC = 28¬∞$.

steps.instruction: Identify equal sides using properties of a rectangle.
steps.working: Opposite sides of a rectangle are equal: $AB = CD$ and $BC = DA$.
All angles in a rectangle are $90¬∞$, so $\angle ABC = \angle CDA = 90¬∞$.

steps.instruction: Match the congruence test: two sides and the included angle.
Write the correspondence and find $\angle DCA$.
steps.working: The correspondence is $A \leftrightarrow C$, $B \leftrightarrow D$, $C \leftrightarrow A$. So $\angle BAC \leftrightarrow \angle DCA$, giving $\angle DCA = 28¬∞$.

answer: $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle CDA$ by SAS, and $\angle DCA = 28¬∞$.
